subject: Provide grammar-constrained generation for Demerzel/Seldon governance outputs

requirements.description: Expose probabilistic grammar service via MCP for governance artifact generation
requirements.detail: Demerzel and Seldon need tars' EBNF grammar engine to constrain LLM outputs when generating governance artifacts. This ensures directives, compliance reports, knowledge packages, and belief states conform to Galactic Protocol schemas without post-hoc validation.
acceptance_criteria: MCP tool: tars_grammar_constrain — accepts a schema reference + prompt, returns grammar-constrained output
MCP tool: tars_grammar_validate — validates existing text against a grammar specification
Supported schemas: directive, compliance-report, belief-snapshot, knowledge-package, learning-outcome, ml-feedback-recommendation

requirements.description: Grammar definitions for all Galactic Protocol message types
requirements.detail: Create EBNF grammar files that match each contract schema in governance/demerzel/schemas/contracts/. These grammars constrain LLM generation to produce valid JSON conforming to the schemas.
Seldon uses tars grammars to generate structured teaching materials and assessment questions. Demerzel uses them to generate conscience reports that follow the weekly report template exactly.
acceptance_criteria: Grammar for weekly conscience report format
Grammar for knowledge transfer curriculum structure
Grammar for assessment question format with answer rubric

requirements.description: Probabilistic weighting for governance grammar productions
requirements.detail: Grammar productions should have probability weights reflecting governance priorities. Constitutional references should be weighted higher than operational details. Harm-related language should trigger careful production selection.
acceptance_criteria: Default weights aligned with constitutional hierarchy (Asimov > Default > Policy > Persona)
Weight profiles per governance context (directive vs. teaching vs. assessment)

constitutional_basis: Article 1 (Truthfulness) — grammar constraints prevent fabricated schema fields
Article 7 (Auditability) — constrained outputs are inherently auditable
Article 8 (Observability) — grammar validation makes output structure observable
